tree: AYUMI TRIO DONE (Julia, 13 commits): σ-phylo REML (clean-LBFGS = the confirmed default) + missing RESPONSES (stress-verified, handles 'many') + BOUNDARY INFERENCE (σ-SD profile CI via confint + bridge). Speed-up exploration PAUSED (Newton not faster than clean-LBFGS; trial deferred). Preparing GLLVM + Ayumi notifications (HELD for go-ahead). R-gate agent finishing; missing PREDICTORS = future FIML.

activity.what: MISSING-MANY stress test (agent) ALL PASS, no breakages: random 10–95% missing, fully-missing species (full tree dim G=78 kept, dropped species stay in the prior), NaN==Missing, order-invariant, drm==bridge in 100% of cells. Sub-0.2 σ-SDs only at genuine data-starvation boundaries. Ayumi's 'many missing' trait is robustly handled.

AYUMI BOUNDARY INFERENCE exposed + tested (e179820): confint(:profile) + drm_bridge_inference now surface the σ-phylo SD boundary-aware CI (resd_sigma, [0.35,0.82], honest [0,x]). 5/5. The Ayumi trio (locscale+REML+boundary) is COMPLETE Julia-side.

AYUMI RESPONSES built (50bb0e5): σ-phylo route dispatched before the missing-response handling, so missing rows weren't dropped. Fixed with an observed-mask drop that KEEPS the full tree. drm + bridge: conv=true, nobs=124/144, σ-SD 0.55. 6/6 test. land-sigma-phylo already had BOTH REML + missing-response — no merge needed.

parallel: drmTMB R-side reml_supported gate relax for the σ-phylo cell (background agent on drmTMB-RELEASE). Scout (w89utibd1) mapping the FIML missing-PREDICTORS track (the 'build all at some point' future work).

RE-verification (522180a): caught a leak MY fix introduced — Inf→1e18 made the penalty sentinel finite, leaking −1e18 into reml_loglik/AIC/BIC on a collinear mean design. Fixed (sentinel→NaN) + regression test. All 12 findings now addressed; REML fully verified.

FIXED all 12 first-pass findings (ff7df6c): BLOCKING boundary crash (0/40 now), HIGH β-coupling (production → clean-LBFGS, Newton experimental), boundary V PD-guard, coupled :REML error guard, doc honesty + boundary regression test.
adversarial verification found 12 REAL bugs my benign-fixture tests missed (1 BLOCKING crash + a β-coupling correctness bug). The gate earned its keep — this is exactly why we ran it.

p=120 LARGE-P MATCH confirmed: safeguarded Newton σSD 0.3684 vs FD-REML 0.3671 (<1%), conv=true in 1 step, 16.5× faster (14.7s vs 242s). Speedup grows with p. vs our own FD-REML — cross-engine still pending.
FIXED the divergence — backtracking line search (monotone descent ⇒ can't diverge) + scale-free convergence (step size in log-SD, not the n-scaling score). The benchmark earned its keep.
benchmark REVEALED the raw Newton diverged at p≥120 (σSD≈7) and step count was fixture-dependent — honest: the '3 steps' was fixture-luck
committed a5bcb3f (REML Newton slice + dev-log finding). NOT pushed (gated)
separate-block (both axes, K=2) Newton — recovers both phylo SDs vs FD-REML; general K-component _glsp_reml_newton
panel PROVED the AI data-quadratic invalid (shrunk BLUP) — fix = observed information

inflight: Verifying: p=120 FD-REML-vs-Newton large-p MATCH (background, FD-REML is the slow ~4.5min part)
Adversarial verification workflow over the committed slice (correctness/robustness/integration/honesty) — running
Then: commit the safeguarded-Newton fix + bench artifacts; coupled K=3 route
Held / user-gated: push, Ayumi reply, GLLVM brief
